negative_cases.why_wrong: Laplace Zp Zero Pole Filter: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to Apply the declared zero/pole transfer to V(in).

Laplace Zp Zero Pole Filter: keeps the main operator syntax but collapses the computed value through the wrong condition, so coarse smoke cases may run while boundary and waveform checks for Use laplace_zp() for zero/pole transfer modeling fail.
Laplace Zp Zero Pole Filter: introduces incorrect initialization or state bias, so startup and reset-sensitive checks fail while the model still compiles and produces activity.

Laplace Zp Zero Pole Filter: offsets the secondary metric or observable path, so the primary waveform may look plausible while calibrated checker evidence for Apply the declared zero/pole transfer to V(in) fails.
